quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Residual pattern"?

options.text: A curved or systematic pattern in the residual plot indicates the linear model is inadequate and a transformation or different model is needed
The variable suspected of influencing or predicting the response variable in a study
An unobserved variable that influences both the explanatory and response variables, creating a false impression of a direct relationship
Neither the subjects nor the researchers measuring outcomes know which treatment each subject received, minimizing both placebo effect and experimenter bias

quiz_questions.explanation: "Residual pattern" is defined as: A curved or systematic pattern in the residual plot indicates the linear model is inadequate and a transformation or different model is needed. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Log transformation"?

options.text: A scatterplot of residuals versus the explanatory variable; used to check whether a linear model is appropriate for the data
(O - E)²/E for each cell; identifies which observed-expected discrepancies drive the overall test result
A measure of the strength and direction of the linear relationship between two quantitative variables; ranges from -1 to +1
Taking log(y) to linearize exponential growth; taking log(x) to linearize logarithmic relationships; taking both for power models

quiz_questions.explanation: "Log transformation" is defined as: Taking log(y) to linearize exponential growth; taking log(x) to linearize logarithmic relationships; taking both for power models. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Exponential regression"?

options.text: Fits y = ab^x by regressing log(y) against x; the slope of the transformed line gives log(b)
P(A and B) = P(A) × P(B|A); the probability of both events equals the probability of the first times the conditional probability of the second
Events that cannot occur simultaneously; P(A and B) = 0, so P(A or B) = P(A) + P(B)
Tests H₀: μ₁ = μ₂ (or μ₁ - μ₂ = 0) using t = (x̄₁ - x̄₂)/√(s₁²/n₁ + s₂²/n₂)

quiz_questions.explanation: "Exponential regression" is defined as: Fits y = ab^x by regressing log(y) against x; the slope of the transformed line gives log(b). The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Power regression"?

options.text: t = (x̄ - μ₀)/(s/√n); replaces the z-statistic when σ is unknown, using sample standard deviation s in the denominator
A study in which the researcher observes and records data without imposing a treatment or intervention on the subjects
Fits y = ax^b by regressing log(y) against log(x); the slope of the transformed line gives b
The difference between Q3 (75th percentile) and Q1 (25th percentile), measuring the spread of the middle 50% of data

quiz_questions.explanation: "Power regression" is defined as: Fits y = ax^b by regressing log(y) against log(x); the slope of the transformed line gives b. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Model selection"?

options.text: For Y = a + bX: E(Y) = a + b·E(X) and Var(Y) = b²·Var(X); adding a constant shifts the mean but does not change spread
Compare R² values, residual plots, and prediction accuracy across candidate models to choose the best-fitting transformation
The fraction of successes in a sample: p̂ = X/n, where X is the number of successes
The variable suspected of influencing or predicting the response variable in a study

quiz_questions.explanation: "Model selection" is defined as: Compare R² values, residual plots, and prediction accuracy across candidate models to choose the best-fitting transformation. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "A curved or systematic pattern in the residual plot indicates the linear model is inadequate and a transformation or different model is needed"?

options.text: Residual pattern
Margin of error (for means)
Stratified random sample
Heavy tails

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Residual pattern".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Taking log(y) to linearize exponential growth; taking log(x) to linearize logarithmic relationships; taking both for power models"?

options.text: Establishing causation
Log transformation
Replication
Standard deviation of a sum/difference

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Log transformation".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Fits y = ab^x by regressing log(y) against x; the slope of the transformed line gives log(b)"?

options.text: Exponential regression
Population regression model
Multiplication rule (general)
Two-way table

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Exponential regression".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Fits y = ax^b by regressing log(y) against log(x); the slope of the transformed line gives b"?

options.text: Power regression
Type II error
Zero in the interval
Variance (s²)

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Power regression".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Compare R² values, residual plots, and prediction accuracy across candidate models to choose the best-fitting transformation"?

options.text: Pooled proportion
Mutually exclusive events
Model selection
Least-squares regression line (LSRL)

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Model selection".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about Transformations and Non-Linear Models is accurate?

options.text: The binomial distribution counts the number of successes in a fixed number of independent trials with constant success probability: P(X=k) = C(n,k)p^k(1-p)^(n-k), with mean np and standard deviation √(np(1-p)).
Categorical Data Analysis examines the distribution and relationships of categorical variables using frequency tables, relative frequency tables, and bar charts.
Transformations and Non-Linear Models in regression address data where the relationship between x and y is not linear.
The test statistic t = d̄/(sd/√n) with df = n-1 (where n is the number of pairs) tests H₀: μd = 0.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Transformations and Non-Linear Models as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which statement correctly describes an aspect of Transformations and Non-Linear Models?

options.text: When residual plots reveal a pattern (curve, fan shape), transformations such as log(y), √y, 1/y, or log(x) can linearize the relationship.
This test is equivalent to testing whether the correlation coefficient ρ equals zero.
The F-test from ANOVA output provides the same conclusion for simple linear regression (F = t²).
The CLT also applies to sample proportions and sums.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Transformations and Non-Linear Models as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which is true specifically about Transformations and Non-Linear Models?

options.text: Tree diagrams and two-way tables provide systematic visual methods for computing conditional probabilities in complex scenarios.
The choice of transformation depends on the curvature pattern, and predictions must be back-transformed to the original scale.

The addition rule for mutually exclusive events (P(A or B) = P(A) + P(B)) and the general addition rule (P(A or B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A and B)) handle unions, while the multiplication rule handles intersections.

The three principles of experimental design are control (using comparison groups), randomization (assigning treatments randomly to reduce bias), and replication (using enough subjects to detect real effects).

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Transformations and Non-Linear Models as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best explains a key idea of Transformations and Non-Linear Models?

options.text: Model comparison using r² and residual plots helps determine which model (linear, exponential, power) best fits the data.
Technology output typically provides b₁, SE(b₁), t, and p-value directly, so interpreting computer regression output is an essential skill.
For any random variables: E(X ± Y) = E(X) ± E(Y), always.
Conditional Probability measures the probability of an event given that another event has occurred: P(A|B) = P(A and B)/P(B).

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Transformations and Non-Linear Models as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which accurately reflects the content on Transformations and Non-Linear Models?

options.text: Hypotheses and P-Values form the logical framework for statistical significance testing.
t-Distributions replace the normal distribution when the population standard deviation σ is unknown and must be estimated by the sample standard deviation s.
Transformations and Non-Linear Models in regression address data where the relationship between x and y is not linear.
Technology output typically provides b₁, SE(b₁), t, and p-value directly, so interpreting computer regression output is an essential skill.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Transformations and Non-Linear Models as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following is a real-world application of Transformations and Non-Linear Models?

options.text: Hospital data examining whether survival rates (survived/died) are conditionally distributed differently across different treatment regimens
Modeling world population growth (exponential) by plotting log(population) vs. year to achieve linearity and project future population
Political campaigns comparing voter support proportions between two demographic groups to target messaging strategies
Tobacco litigation using decades of observational evidence (cohort studies, dose-response relationships) to establish smoking as a cause of lung cancer when experiments were unethical

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Transformations and Non-Linear Models.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which example demonstrates the practical relevance of Transformations and Non-Linear Models?

options.text: Demonstrating the CLT with simulations: rolling dice (uniform distribution) and plotting the mean of 30 rolls—the distribution of means is approximately normal despite the uniform population
Astronomy: the power relationship between a planet's orbital period and distance from the sun (Kepler's Third Law: T² ∝ r³) linearized using log-log transformation
Economists checking LINE conditions before publishing a regression model of how education level predicts income
A nutritionist testing whether the mean sodium content of a product exceeds 500 mg using a sample of 15 packages and a one-sample t-test

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Transformations and Non-Linear Models.

quiz_questions.question_text: How is Transformations and Non-Linear Models applied in real-world contexts?

options.text: Identifying non-linear trends in economic data (GDP growth vs. time) when residual plots show curved patterns requiring transformation
Public health research testing whether smoking status (smoker/non-smoker) is independent of lung disease status (yes/no) in a random sample of adults
Market research testing whether product preference is associated with age group to determine whether different demographics need different marketing strategies
Economics: linearizing the diminishing returns relationship between advertising spending and sales using a logarithmic transformation of spending

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Transformations and Non-Linear Models.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which scenario illustrates the principles of Transformations and Non-Linear Models?

options.text: Demonstrating the CLT with simulations: rolling dice (uniform distribution) and plotting the mean of 30 rolls—the distribution of means is approximately normal despite the uniform population
Public health research testing whether smoking status (smoker/non-smoker) is independent of lung disease status (yes/no) in a random sample of adults
Analyzing biological measurements (human heights, birth weights) that naturally approximate normal distributions to identify atypical values
Modeling world population growth (exponential) by plotting log(population) vs. year to achieve linearity and project future population

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Transformations and Non-Linear Models.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best shows Transformations and Non-Linear Models in practice?

options.text: Astronomy: the power relationship between a planet's orbital period and distance from the sun (Kepler's Third Law: T² ∝ r³) linearized using log-log transformation
Clinical trials comparing cure rates between drug and placebo groups to determine whether the drug is more effective than no treatment
Polling organizations reporting margin of error, which depends on the standard error σ/√n, explaining why larger polls are more precise
Political polling organizations using stratified sampling by demographic groups (age, region, party) to produce accurate election forecasts

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Transformations and Non-Linear Models.
